verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 is canonically ER-resident but translocates to the cell surface under ER stress and in cancer cells. It localizes to the outer leaflet as a peripheral protein via interactions with GPI-anchored and other surface proteins, and exhibits phospholipid-binding capacity that enables surface association. Documented in cancer ecto-presentation and ER-stress-induced contexts. Therapeutic targeting programs (antibodies, small molecules) engage surface-displayed HSPA5 in cancer patients.
